channel.title: PYT Radar
channel.description: Podcast & YouTube Tracking â automated episode digest. Tracking mentions of Karen Bass, Nithya Raman, and more across podcasts and YouTube.

item.title: Fly on the Wall: David Spade blames Karen Bass for Hollywood decline
item.description:
Fly on the Wall with Dana Carvey and David Spade
"Adam Friedland Joins the Old Boys Club" â Published March 19, 2026

Match: Karen Bass (mentioned, not a guest)

Sentiment: Negative

David Spade directly named Mayor Karen Bass and Governor Newsom as bearing responsibility for the decline of Hollywood production in LA, citing the bankruptcy of CBS Radford Studio Center. Dana Carvey called the Hollywood studio system dying and argued for tax breaks and union negotiations.

Key moment at 20:54: "I was on the lot at CBS Radford when we were doing Just Shoot Me... just filed for bankruptcy. The lot, terrifying in L.A. Thanks, Karen Bass. Thanks, Gavin." â David Spade

â¶ Watch Episode | ð Summary | ð Full Transcript

item.title: Culture Shock Radio: This 92-minute episode of Culture Shock Radio mentions Karen Bass.
item.description:
Culture Shock Radio
"Karen Bass Endorsement Under Fire After LA Times Criticism | Adam Carolla Show News - Daily Podcast" – Published 2026-03-20

Match: Karen Bass (guest)

Sentiment: Neutral

This 92-minute episode of Culture Shock Radio mentions Karen Bass.

Key moment at 04:44: "Well, this is— I mean, this is sort of what we're talking about, which is The politicians, Sidney nut job we just saw, and Karen Bass, this isn't something they're interested in. They're not interested in it. It's not sexy, it's not fun. They don't get to accuse people of being racist. You know, it's nothing they're interested in. Like, honestly, I am interested in it. So if I was a politician, I wouldn't do the racial hustling. I'd be a dog with a bone and burying these wires. You know, that's all I would talk about. They are a bunch of dingbat chicks who have no interest in this. So of course we don't get the wires buried. And we have a bunch of politicians that are much more interested in saving the milk thistle weed than we do burying the wires." – Speaker B

▶ Watch Episode

item.title: Adam Carolla: This 14-minute episode of Adam Carolla mentions Karen Bass.
item.description:
Adam Carolla
"Karen Bass Blames LA Heat Wave on Climate Change | Adam Carolla Reacts!" – Published 2026-03-19

Match: Karen Bass (guest)

Sentiment: Neutral

This 14-minute episode of Adam Carolla mentions Karen Bass.

Key moment at 00:00: "I have a clip that made me laugh, which was our mayor Karen Bass was talking about how hot it was. I always laugh that it's unseasonably hot in LA and they have to give these PSAs about what to do when it's hot." – Speaker A

▶ Watch Episode

item.title: The Moment LIVE: This 19-minute episode of The Moment LIVE mentions Nithya Raman.
item.description:
The Moment LIVE
"Who's Going to Fix LA? Nithya Raman on the Budget Crisis, Housing & 2028 Olympics | The Moment LIVE" – Published 2026-03-23

Match: Nithya Raman (mentioned)

Sentiment: Neutral

This 19-minute episode of The Moment LIVE mentions Nithya Raman.

Key moment at 00:00: "We have with us City Councilmember Nithya Raman, who is running for mayor of Los Angeles. Nithya is a Los Angeles City Council member representing District 4. She's first elected in 2020. She has focused on housing affordability, tenant protections, and unhoused folks. She's an urban planner by training and the co-founder of SELA— maybe it's SELA, or do you think we say the letters? Neighborhood Homeless Coalition. I think, I think we have her." – Speaker A

▶ Watch Episode

item.title: The Moment LIVE: This 53-minute episode of The Moment LIVE mentions Nithya Raman.
item.description:
The Moment LIVE
"Justice for César Chávez Victims? Taylor Frankie Paul & Nithya Raman for LA Mayor | The Moment LIVE" – Published 2026-03-21

Match: Nithya Raman (mentioned)

Sentiment: Neutral

This 53-minute episode of The Moment LIVE mentions Nithya Raman.

Key moment at 02:14: "It's like, I don't want to do it anyway. Well, well, we're here, we're alive. I'm happy to be alive, you know. Um, let's— there's, uh, a story that blew my mind this week. I think it blew a lot of people's minds. You and I were in the room together when we found out the New York Times— well, actually, first let me tell you, today we're going to talk about the New York Times Cesar Chavez investigation, which has upended years of history and opened all sorts of questions about leftist spaces and women and Dolores Huertas. Um, then we're going to talk about Taylor Frankie Paul, who was supposed to be The Bachelorette this season. That season got canceled on ABC 3 days before air because of domestic violence videos that were leaked. Um, it was harrowing. And then we're going to talk to Nithya Raman, who is a council member here in LA running for mayor, and she's sort of LA's Zoran, maybe some are saying. And, uh, she— we're really excited to talk to her about, uh, the campaign that she's running. I think there's a lot of people who are frustrated with how things are going on in LA, and, um, you know, Nithya versus Spencer Pratt. So we're gonna get into it with her." – Speaker A

▶ Watch Episode
